message: It was Saturday afternoon... For some reason, I was riding the train.
"Let's ride this train to the last stop."
It kind of felt like Takashima-san forced me into this.

name: Yuki
message: "By the way, where is the last stop?"

name: Zakuro
message: "The amusement park. Have you ever been there?"

name: Yuki
message: "Is that right? I haven't been there. I didn't even know there was an amusement park there."

name: Zakuro
message: "Is that so..."
For a moment, her voice sounded kind of sad.
Did I say something wrong?

name: Yuki
message: "U-Uh, have you been there before?"

name: Zakuro
message: "I... I've been there once before."

name: Yuki
message: "Oh, really? With your parents?"

name: Zakuro
message: "No. I was with the person I loved the most in the world."

name: Yuki
message: "That's the first I've heard of that. There's someone that you're in love with?"

name: Zakuro
message: "Yes, there was."

name: Yuki
message: "Was? Why are you using the past tense?"

name: Zakuro
message: "Everything about the person I knew, even their face, has changed."

name: Yuki
message: "Even their face changed?"

name: Zakuro
message: "Yes, so the person I loved isn't in this world anymore."

name: Yuki
message: "You mean..."
Maybe it was an accident, or sickness, or something...

name: Yuki
message: "D-Did I ask something I shouldn't have?"

name: Zakuro
message: "No, I don't mind at all. It's not your fault. But leaving that aside..."
Takashima-san pointed her finger out the window.

name: Zakuro
message: "I can see the last station. Look, there's the amusement park."
The last stop was right in front of the amusement park.
I'd been riding this train for years, but I never noticed that it stopped there.
It was the last stop, so I saw "Amusement Park Bound" every time I rode this train, but I probably didn't care.
The doors opened at the last station.
People got off much more slowly than they would at any other stop.
The doors stayed open for a long time at the last stop. There was no need to rush like when you're trying to get off at one of the intermediate stops.
At the "Amusement Park" station, everyone left slowly, idling nearby.
Next stop: a land of wonder.
Though this "land of wonder" was old and faded.
The people were all laughing as they stepped into that cheap wonderland.

name: Zakuro
message: "Shouldn't we get off here too?"

name: Yuki
message: "That's why you brought me here, isn't it?"

name: Zakuro
message: "Yes. That's right, but it's just a place where I have a lot of memories. It probably doesn't mean anything to you, so I don't mind if you would rather go home."

name: Yuki
message: "There's no point going home now."

name: Zakuro
message: "Really? Just being with you like this is meaningful enough for me."

name: Yuki
message: "There you go again. You shouldn't make those jokes so casually."

name: Zakuro
message: "So I shouldn't make those jokes because they make Tsukasa-san and Kagami-san act strange?"

name: Yuki
message: "Yeah, pretty much."

name: Zakuro
message: "But it's a bit upsetting that you would call them jokes, Yuki-sama."

name: Yuki
message: "Huh?"

name: Zakuro
message: "I really do enjoy spending time alone with you, Yuki... even though we can't get away from those other two very often."

name: Yuki
message: "Get away from them?"

name: Zakuro
message: "If Kagami-san and Tsukasa-san knew the truth about what we're doing today, things probably wouldn't end peacefully."

name: Yuki
message: "Th-There you go again. Those two are just messing around."

name: Zakuro
message: "You're still saying that it's just a... joke, I guess."

name: Yuki
message: "Yeah, exactly."

name: Zakuro
message: "Then go on some rides with me here at the amusement park. Just play along, as a joke."

name: Yuki
message: "Oh, sure. That was the plan anyway."

name: Zakuro
message: "Thank you."

name: Yuki
message: "Well, let's go, then."

name: Zakuro
message: "Yes."
We walked around together.
Our shoes echoed off the hard concrete.

name: Yuki
message: "I wonder how long it's been since I last had shaved ice."

name: Zakuro
message: "Is it good?"

name: Yuki
message: "Yeah. It's just ice, but it's really good."

name: Zakuro
message: "Heheh. I dragged you all the way out here, so this is the least I could do."

name: Yuki
message: "You really didn't need to pay for mine too."

name: Zakuro
message: "No, I did. I knew you would say that, because you're so kind."

name: Yuki
message: "That's not true."

name: Zakuro
message: "Heheh. Really? I think you're kind just for saying that."

name: Yuki
message: "I dunno about that..."

name: Yuki
message: "Anyway, there are a lot of people here, even though it's such a shabby amusement park. I heard places like these usually operate in the red nowadays."

name: Zakuro
message: "How crowded it is just shows how amazing this amusement park is."

name: Yuki
message: "Maybe so."
There was a fake castle, and mannequins were rusted. The whole place was a bunch of silly dreams, all thrown haphazardly together.
It was a world where various dreams were put together without any unifying theme or logic.
People laughed and played in such a cheap world. Happy people.
As I walked among those people, I felt like I had turned into a ghost.
I felt a strange sensation, as if my feet weren't actually touching the ground...
As if a dream had become real...

name: Zakuro
message: "Today is my treat, so let's enjoy ourselves."
That strange feeling was dispelled by Takashima-san's smile.

name: Yuki
message: "Oh, sure."
A herd of smiles surrounded me, gathered here in this cheap world.
I was saved from the feeling of floating by the person at my side.

name: Yuki
message: "Oh, but you really don't need to pay for me. Anyway, we're here, so let's enjoy ourselves. I actually haven't been to an amusement park since I was a child."

name: Zakuro
message: "Is that right? Not since you were a child? Oh, by the way, Yuki-sama."

name: Yuki
message: "What?"

name: Zakuro
message: "Can we call this a date?"

name: Yuki
message: "Huh? I don't really mind, but why?"

name: Zakuro
message: "Oh, nothing. Just something for me to remember you by."

name: Yuki
message: "Remember me?"

name: Zakuro
message: "You know, tomorrow is the 19th."

name: Yuki
message: "Yeah, it is, but so what?"

name: Zakuro
message: "The date for the stargazing was set for the 19th. We will search for the beginning and end of tomorrow's night sky."

name: Yuki
message: "Yeah, we will. I hope we find it this time."

name: Zakuro
message: "Yes, but if we do, we probably can't be together the next day, on the 20th."

name: Yuki
message: "Huh? Wh-What do you mean?"
Did she mean that she was the world girl? That she thought she was going to return to the sky on the 19th?
But if she really tried to do that...

name: Yuki
message: "E-Excuse me, Takashima-san."

name: Zakuro
message: "Heheh. What is it? Did you think I was planning to jump off the roof on the 19th?"

name: Yuki
message: "Uh, no. How should I put it?"

name: Zakuro
message: "Don't worry. I won't do anything like that."

name: Zakuro
message: "That was a joke. Just a joke. It's not true that we won't be able to be together after the 20th."

name: Yuki
message: "Oh, really? Don't scare me like that."

name: Zakuro
message: "I'm sorry."

name: Yuki
message: "You know, the other day..."

name: Zakuro
message: "The other day?"
Those words tumbled out of my mouth almost by accident. I hesitated, not knowing if I should finish the sentence, but I had to ask her about it.

name: Yuki
message: "I saw you dropping a doll at school. You told me that ritual was supposed to be done when the Summer Triangle was visible, so..."

name: Zakuro
message: "July 16th, 2012, 5:58 PM. Right?"
Why did she remember the exact time? That was the same time that Ayana said it was.
The time was accurate down to the minute.
Maybe there was some meaning in the exact time she did it.

name: Yuki
message: "Yeah, at that time, you dropped the doll from Building C, right?"

name: Zakuro
message: "You saw it, huh?"

name: Yuki
message: "Was I not supposed to?"

name: Zakuro
message: "It doesn't really matter. I was just dropping the doll."

name: Yuki
message: "But why did you drop it at that kind of time? Wasn't that a way of searching for something in the sky?"

name: Zakuro
message: "Yes, that's right."

name: Yuki
message: "But the landmark, the Summer Triangle, wasn't visible at that time."

name: Zakuro
message: "You're right. I guess it wasn't."

name: Yuki
message: "Then why did you drop the doll at that time?"

name: Zakuro
message: "I guess you could call it the borderline."

name: Yuki
message: "The borderline?"

name: Zakuro
message: "Yes."

name: Yuki
message: "Um, Ayana-san said it was supposed to have an effect on another world..."

name: Zakuro
message: "Otonashi Ayana..."
Takashima-san's expression suddenly disappeared.

name: Yuki
message: "Wh-What's the matter?"

name: Zakuro
message: "Otonashi Ayana, you said? So something like her can exist even in this kind of place."

name: Yuki
message: "Even in this kind of place?"

name: Zakuro
message: "But that was inevitable. Now I understand. I can understand why something like her would exist even here."
I was getting goosebumps for some reason. What the heck was this conversation we were having?
I'd been perspiring for a while now. It was a cold sweat.
Takashima-san's expression was dead. I'd never seen a face like that.
Why would her expression change like that when talking about Ayana?

name: Zakuro
message: "But Otonashi-san... Now that I think about it, there were parts of her that were kind, too."

name: Zakuro
message: "The fate I've chosen can't be changed now."

name: Zakuro
message: "Whether it is inevitable or just coincidence isn't a distinction that a human can make."

name: Yuki
message: "What are you talking about?"

name: Zakuro
message: "The last words Otonashi-san said to me."

name: Yuki
message: "The last?"

name: Zakuro
message: "Ah, I'm sorry!"

name: Zakuro
message: "Y-You see, I had an argument with her. Those were the words she said when we parted."

name: Yuki
message: "I-Is that what it was?"
So Takashima-san and Otonashi-san knew each other.

Now that I thought about it, they did both kind of fit into the 
"mysterious girl" mold.

name: Yuki
message: "Takashima-san, you had an argument with Ayana-san?"

name: Zakuro
message: "That's right. Please don't worry too much about what I just said, nor about the incident with the doll."

name: Zakuro
message: "When I dropped the doll from Building C, that was just practice."

name: Yuki
message: "Practice?"

name: Zakuro
message: "Yes. I haven't dropped a doll from the roof in a while, so I was worried I might be out of practice."

name: Yuki
message: "Can you get 'out of practice' at something like that?"

name: Zakuro
message: "Yes. It's rather difficult. You have to throw it so that it floats in the air."

name: Yuki
message: "But it's never floated before, has it?"

name: Zakuro
message: "It hasn't."

name: Yuki
message: "Then how can you practice?"

name: Zakuro
message: "It doesn't float because the sky is wrong. With the correct technique, the doll will float in the right sky."

name: Zakuro
message: "But if you can't throw it correctly, it won't float even in the right sky."

name: Zakuro
message: "And then we would never find the beginning and end of the sky."

name: Yuki
message: "I-I guess that makes sense."
I guess for all these "mysterious" people, it must be a pain following all these "mysterious" rules.
Takashima-san's face suddenly softened.
When she looked at me, the smile had returned to her lips.

name: Yuki
message: "What is it?"

name: Zakuro
message: "This is nothing like that time, when I was so nervous. Right now, I'm totally relaxed, and I'm enjoying this calm, peaceful, happy moment."

name: Yuki
message: "Are you talking about the person you came with the last time you were here?"

name: Zakuro
message: "Ah, yes. That's right."

name: Yuki
message: "There's really no comparison there, though. You were nervous because you were with the person you liked."

name: Zakuro
message: "But I like you too."

name: Yuki
message: "But that's something different. Not 'love,' just plain old 'like.'"

name: Zakuro
message: "If that's how you want to put it, I loved the person I was here with last, and I love you too."
Now she's saying it too. Jeez.

name: Yuki
message: "But still, there's no way you would get nervous around me, since we're both girls."

name: Zakuro
message: "Heheh. Who cares about that? We still have time to enjoy this wonderland!"

name: Yuki
message: "Wonderland, huh? What a cheap wonderland it is."

name: Zakuro
message: "Dreams are nice because they're cheap. If they weren't cheap, they wouldn't be dreams. What do you think about that?"

name: Zakuro
message: "We rode a lot of different ones, huh?"

name: Yuki
message: "Yeah. Are there any others you want to try?"

name: Zakuro
message: "Now that you mention it, there's a boat ride here."

name: Yuki
message: "Oh, is there?"

name: Zakuro
message: "How does that sound? Does the boat sound like fun?"

name: Yuki
message: "That one?"

name: Zakuro
message: "Yes, that ride looks like fun."

name: Yuki
message: "Spirit room: 'The End Sky'..."

name: Zakuro
message: "That's a strange name. It obviously means 'the end of the sky,' but it's written 'The End Sky.'"

name: Yuki
message: "I wonder what it is. It says it's a spirit room, so it's probably one of those scary attractions."

name: Zakuro
message: "I'm not really sure, but it looks odd, doesn't it? It says it's a 'spirit room,' not a 'haunted house.' It sounds kind of creepy."

name: Yuki
message: "Yeah. Let's check it out."

name: Zakuro
message: "Okay."
The building had a bizarre structure.
That was the one thing that made me uncomfortable. Other than that, it looked like an ordinary attraction.
It was one of those attractions where you ride around in a gondola. I couldn't tell if the rails rusted or what, but they were making an unpleasant noise.
We sat in the gondola and looked at various worlds that passed us by, each of which had been neatly demarcated from the last.
At first, we were surrounded by never-ending darkness.
It felt like we were rising and falling, or even moving from side to side sometimes, but we couldn't really tell for sure what was happening in the dark.

name: Yuki
message: "Wh-What is this?"

name: Yuki
message: "What the heck? Are we in a convenience store? Did we go the wrong way?"

name: Zakuro
message: "That can't be it. Why would anyone come to a convenience store on a creepy gondola like this? Besides, there aren't any customers or employees."

name: Yuki
message: "There really isn't anyone here. It's kinda scary how dark it is, too. If something popped out right now, it might actually scare me."
The gondola slowly passed through the deserted store.
I was expecting someone dressed up like a ghost to pop out at any moment, but nothing happened.
The thin layer of dust on all the shelves was a bit eerie.

name: Yuki
message: "Hm... Do you think they would mind if I took a piece of candy?"

name: Zakuro
message: "You can't do that. It's not like there's a sign that says 'free samples' or anything."

name: Yuki
message: "But the security cameras aren't even turned on."

name: Zakuro
message: "That's not the problem here..."
I grabbed a bag of potato chips from a nearby shelf.
The expiration date was July 20th, 1999.

name: Yuki
message: "Ahaha... This is an antique."

name: Zakuro
message: "Let's stop this."
It was starting to make sense. The scenery didn't just look old. It really was old.
What the heck was the designer thinking when he made this ride?
A deserted store. It definitely was creepy, but as an attraction in an amusement park, it was kind of lacking.
The rusty rails squeaked as the gondola turned toward a door behind the register.
I looked at the register.
Something was scribbled on a piece of paper there.
Just one sentence.

Title of this piece
"Convenience Store End"

name: Yuki
message: "This piece? Convenience Store End? What does that mean?

name: Zakuro
message: "Maybe it means that this is the end of the convenience store?"

name: Yuki
message: "The end of the convenience store, huh?"

name: Yuki
message: "What is this supposed to be? Some sort of commentary on consumerism?"

name: Zakuro
message: "I don't know. That seems kind of complicated."
The gondola skirted the wall and entered a staircase. We watched the convenience store recede below us as we ascended.
The gondola went up the endless stairs.
I was a bit surprised that it could go up the steps. It was quite the agile machine.

name: Zakuro
message: "This is a really long stairway, isn't it?"

name: Yuki
message: "It's just stairs and stairs, stretching on forever."

name: Zakuro
message: "Oh, I can see the end."
The staircase that we thought was about to end doubled back in the opposite direction, and after we passed that...

name: Yuki
message: "Was the building really this big?"

name: Zakuro
message: "No. From the outside, it didn't look this big..."

name: Yuki
message: "Wh-What?"

name: Zakuro
message: "It's pitch black."

name: Yuki
message: "What is this?"

name: Zakuro
message: "It looks like the gondola got on an elevator."

name: Yuki
message: "What kind of ride is this?"

name: Zakuro
message: "This part looks like an apartment building."

name: Yuki
message: "An apartment building..."

name: Zakuro
message: "Oh, the gondola is moving again."
It moved steadily through the concrete hallway. That was a barren, lifeless hallway, and there was no telling where it would lead.

name: Yuki
message: "Hey. This is a hallway, right? We have to be in an apartment complex..."

name: Zakuro
message: "Yes, probably."

name: Yuki
message: "Then why are there curtains on the hallway windows?"

name: Zakuro
message: "Huh?"
The windows of the once-modern hallway had at some point changed into the decrepit windows of a condemned apartment.
I had thought that we were still in the hallway, but maybe the gondola turned into one of the rooms without either of us noticing.

name: Zakuro
message: "This is a room, isn't it?"

name: Yuki
message: "It looks so old."

name: Zakuro
message: "Ah."

name: Yuki
message: "Would you call this the living room?"

name: Zakuro
message: "It feels like we're back in the Shouwa era."
There was an engraved plate on the table.

Title of this piece
"Living Room End"

name: Yuki
message: "What's this? Maybe it really is some sort of criticism of modern culture."

name: Zakuro
message: "Maybe it's trying to express a father's fear that the tight-knit family unit is falling apart."
The sound of grinding rust filled the room as the gondola advanced forward. The entire scene felt kind of surreal, or rather, downright eerie.
The calendar and clock were both stopped at the same point.

name: Yuki
message: "July of 1999, again."
What was up with that date? It must have been the date when this attraction was made.
We left the living room and emerged into another staircase, except this time it was a small set of stairs like in someone's house.
It was really creepy.

name: Yuki
message: "Huh? Those stairs led right into a classroom."

name: Zakuro
message: "The living room was connected to the classroom, huh? Heheh. If you lived in a house like that, you would never be late, would you?"

name: Yuki
message: "But on the other hand, your teacher could come to your room and ask, 'Are you doing your homework?'"

name: Zakuro
message: "Ahaha. You're right, that might be annoying."
A plate was set upon the podium.

Title of this piece
"End of the Classroom"
A convenience store, a living room, and then a classroom.
I see. So the designer was trying to bring out a feeling of unease.
There was no one in all these places where people should be, and the riders feel like intruders for being in places where they shouldn't be.

name: Yuki
message: "I see. So that's what it meant by 'spirit room.'"

name: Zakuro
message: "What do you mean?"

name: Yuki
message: "All of the rooms are places that exist so that people can live in them. And when you suddenly take the people out of them, and look at all of these places from this gondola..."

name: Yuki
message: "We get to look into these people's lifestyles, as if those lifestyles had been amputated and preserved in a jar."

name: Yuki
message: "I think we're the spirits on this ride."

name: Zakuro
message: "So there won't be any ghosts popping out and scaring us?"

name: Yuki
message: "I don't think they're going to do anything crude like that."

name: Yuki
message: "If any ghosts appeared, we would go back to being human."
The gondola left the classroom and went out into the hallway.
Just how big was this building?

name: Zakuro
message: "It's a really long ride, isn't it?"

name: Yuki
message: "Yeah. It's just showing us all these deserted scenes, but it's scary how big this place is."
The gondola kept moving through the hallway. The rust was causing a high-pitched whine.
The gondola advanced past the classroom and... stopped.

name: Zakuro
message: "It stopped."

name: Yuki
message: "Yeah."
A bell rang behind us as a door closed.
The gondola rotated around, and...

name: Yuki
message: "Somehow we ended up in another elevator."

name: Zakuro
message: "Yes. It really is a huge building, isn't it?"

Title of this piece
"The Final Abode"

name: Yuki
message: "'The Final Abode'... That's kind of a gross name for a hospital."

name: Zakuro
message: "By the 'The Final Abode,' they mean the place where you live while waiting to die, I guess."

name: Yuki
message: "You might be right. Most people die in the hospital, but that's a creepy way of saying it."
Nevertheless, this was a hell of a design.
The gondola started to move into the bed.

name: Zakuro
message: "Whoa, we're going to run into it."

name: Yuki
message: "Whoa!"
The gondola went into the bed.
What kind of structure did this ride have?

name: Zakuro
message: "It's dark, isn't it?"

name: Yuki
message: "Yeah. It's pitch black."

name: Zakuro
message: "Oh, there's a light."
The blue sky spread out above us.
And...
When we looked down...
There was another plate.

Title of this piece
"The End Sky"

name: Yuki
message: "I see. So that's what it was."
A tremor ran up my spine, twisting my face into a jagged smile.
Normally, you were supposed to be scared when you were inside a haunted house, not when you left it.
But for some reason, this one was different.
It felt as if everything up to this point was a prelude.
The creepy feeling from all the deserted places where people should've been, and all the places where we had no right to be, culminated beneath this sky.
Just like that, the gondola stopped.
It seemed like the ride should return to the beginning for the next person, but if this wasn't the end, it would be like the plate was lying.

name: Yuki
message: "Th-That was kind of weird."

name: Zakuro
message: "Heheh. I suppose so."

name: Yuki
message: "Well then, how about we ride something fun next?"

name: Zakuro
message: "That sounds good. It would be nice to ride something more relaxing."
Really, who the heck would make a ride like that?
I doubted there was any particular meaning to it. But still, it wasn't a very pleasant experience.

name: Yuki
message: "A ride where you can experience the feeling of being a ghost..."

name: Zakuro
message: "Yuki-sama, look! There's a boat over here!"

name: Yuki
message: "Oh, sure. Just a sec."

name: Zakuro
message: "Yuki-sama, how does that sound? Would you ride the boat with me?"

name: Yuki
message: "Oh, that might be nice. After that weird ride, a boat ride would probably feel good."
Creak... Creak...
Creak... Creak...

name: Zakuro
message: "I'm sorry about this. I'm making you row, even though I'm the maid."

name: Yuki
message: "It's nothing. Ahaha..."
At first, Takashima-san said she was going to row, but with her at the oars, we'd hardly moved at all.

name: Yuki
message: "I'm not very good at this, so just say something if you're feeling seasick."

name: Zakuro
message: "I'm fine. There's no way I would get sick from your rowing."

name: Yuki
message: "How can you say that with such certainty?"

name: Zakuro
message: "Ahaha. I wonder why."

name: Zakuro
message: "The breeze on this lake is always nice and cool."

name: Yuki
message: "This is the first time I've been here, so I wouldn't know."

name: Zakuro
message: "It feels really good. I think it's wonderful."

name: Yuki
message: "Yeah, the water draws the cool breeze over the lake, doesn't it?"

name: Zakuro
message: "It feels so nice..."

name: Zakuro
message: "The water looks like a mirror reflecting the sky, like we're in a boat flying through the heavens."

name: Yuki
message: "I see what you mean, but that would be kinda scary."

name: Zakuro
message: "Do you think so? I think it feels good to walk through the clouds."

name: Yuki
message: "Ahaha. You say that as if you've actually done it before."

name: Yuki
message: "I've never walked on a cloud before, so I wouldn't know. I just think it's kind of scary to imagine us being up in the sky."

name: Zakuro
message: "Scary, is it? That's the opposite of what you said last time."

name: Yuki
message: "Last time?"

name: Zakuro
message: "Oh, it's nothing. I'm just talking to myself."
Creak... Creak...
Creak... Creak...

name: Zakuro
message: "Heheh."

name: Yuki
message: "Hmm? What's up?"

name: Zakuro
message: "Oh, it's just... Look, if I touch the water, it's like I can reach out and touch the sky, I guess. That's all."

name: Yuki
message: "Hehehe. That's interesting."

name: Zakuro
message: "That's something I heard from the person I loved."

name: Yuki
message: "Oh? He was a poet, wasn't he?"

name: Zakuro
message: "He was a poet, a swordsman, a philosopher, and a pioneer of aerodynamics."
We were on the train back home.
It felt nice. Our languid spirits swayed back and forth with the movement of the train.
And then...
Music.
Takashima-san looked like she was enjoying listening to my music.
I had her listen to a few songs, judging what genres she liked from her expression. It seemed like Takashima-san enjoyed the classical music.
I told her that this recording was from a New Year's concert they held every year in a foreign country.
Their conductor was lauded as a genius, and he led the most famous philharmonic in the world.
Waltz.
Polka.
March.
A dancing melody.
The swaying of the train seemed to match the rhythm of the songs.
I felt her softly put her hand on my hand that was holding the music player.
Fittingly, the name on the screen was Vergnugungszug, "Pleasure Train."
Ours was just a ride across town, not a journey, but the relaxed feeling was the same. The song almost made the scenery outside look like it was glowing.

name: Zakuro
message: "Do you like classical music?"

name: Yuki
message: "Well, my mother used to be really strict, and she made me practice the piano."

name: Zakuro
message: "You can play the piano, then?"

name: Yuki
message: "Yeah, as long as it's not too hard, I can manage."

name: Zakuro
message: "Then could you play for me, tonight?"

name: Yuki
message: "Tonight?"

name: Zakuro
message: "Yes. I know a store where they have a piano."

name: Yuki
message: "Is that so?"

name: Zakuro
message: "Yes, though it's a place I heard about from the person I loved."

name: Yuki
message: "Oh, is it?"

name: Zakuro
message: "Would you mind going with me?"

name: Yuki
message: "I'd be glad to."

name: Yuki
message: "This kind of looks like a bar or something."

name: Zakuro
message: "Don't worry about that. Here, sit on this seat."

name: Yuki
message: "You're right. There is a piano."

name: Zakuro
message: "Yes. Here."
Takashima-san handed me a key.

name: Yuki
message: "What's this?"

name: Zakuro
message: "The key to the piano... This bar's piano."

name: Yuki
message: "Why do you have a key? Do you work here or something?"

name: Zakuro
message: "Heheh. Who knows?"

name: Yuki
message: "The name is... Hakushuukyou Bar. Hmm?"
I guess all bars really do look the same. This was my first time in the place, but it felt oddly familiar.

name: Yuki
message: "Is it all right if I play this piano?"

name: Zakuro
message: "There's no problem at all. Please, play the piano."

name: Yuki
message: "Ah, sure."
I played a few notes. It was pretty well tuned.
Actually, the piano felt like it was really easy to play. It seemed that the piano and I were compatible.

name: Yuki
message: "Is there anything you'd like to hear?"

name: Zakuro
message: "Yes. For the first one, Erik Satie. Le Picadilly would be nice."

name: Yuki
message: "Oh, you listen to Satie. Good taste."

name: Zakuro
message: "That's right. Enjoying Satie is in good taste. He was a great composer."

name: Yuki
message: "Oh, that's rare. Are you singing your own praises now?"

name: Zakuro
message: "I guess I am."

name: Yuki
message: "What a coincidence, though. I like Satie too."
I spread my fingers over the keys.
I closed my eyes for a moment, and then...
My fingers danced.
As I struck the keys, layers of different tones flowed out from the piano.
The music wove a tapestry of good cheer.
It was a happy rhythm.
Bright.
Ornate.
And somehow nostalgic.
It felt simple and candid.
Satie's music blends into the human lifestyle like a piece of furniture.
Takashima-san listened quietly.
I was having fun playing.

name: Yuki
message: "How was it?"

name: Zakuro
message: "It was wonderful."

name: Yuki
message: "I had fun, too. I haven't played in a long time."
And I didn't feel rusty at all.
I hadn't been practicing on the piano at home, so I thought that I would mess up, but...

name: Zakuro
message: "Could you play one more?"

name: Yuki
message: "Yeah, sure. What song?"

name: Zakuro
message: "I would like to hear your favorite song."

name: Yuki
message: "My favorite?"

name: Zakuro
message: "Yes, your number one favorite song."

name: Yuki
message: "My favorite, huh?"
I faced the piano again and closed my eyes.
I slowly opened my hands, and...
I began to play.
I played my favorite song.
Takashima-san listened with a happy expression on her face.
I had to wonder if she knew this song.
If she liked Satie, she might have heard it before. It was a famous song.
"The Dreamy Fish." That was the title.
It was about the dream a fish living in the ocean might have. That was the image the melody evoked.
They say Satie's titles were often different from the actual content of the pieces.
He didn't want people's impressions to be affected by the title he gave it.
That was why a lot of his compositions had strange titles.
Embryons desseches. Deux preludes pour un chien. Sonatine bureaucratique. Trois morceaux en forme de poire. Tons of songs like that.
But it felt like this song really was about a fish dreaming.

name: Yuki
message: "How was it?"

name: Yuki
message: "Wh-What's wrong?"

name: Zakuro
message: "Yes... Thank you very much."

name: Yuki
message: "Huh? A-Are you crying?"

name: Zakuro
message: "No... I'm not crying."

name: Zakuro
message: "No, just... thank you."

name: Zakuro
message: "This semester is going to end soon."

name: Yuki
message: "Yeah it is, although we skipped today."

name: Zakuro
message: "Yep. So with today, it's over."

name: Yuki
message: "It's over?"

name: Zakuro
message: "Yes, that's why I dragged you along to the amusement park."

name: Yuki
message: "Wh-What do you mean? I don't understand what you're saying."

name: Zakuro
message: "At the end of this semester, I'm transferring to a different school."

name: Yuki
message: "Huh?"

name: Zakuro
message: "I'll only be here in this city until the 20th, when the semester ends."

name: Yuki
message: "R-Really?"

name: Zakuro
message: "Yes. I've almost completely moved out already, but I wanted to at least stay and finish the semester."

name: Yuki
message: "Oh... I see."

name: Zakuro
message: "So tomorrow is the last day I can stay at your house."

name: Zakuro
message: "I have to leave this city the day after tomorrow."

name: Yuki
message: "Why did you stay until now, then?"

name: Yuki
message: "It sounds like your parents have already moved. It must have been hard to convince them to let you stay."

name: Zakuro
message: "Yes. I insisted they let me stay at least until the semester ended."

name: Yuki
message: "Why did you do that? Was there something you still had to do before leaving?"

name: Zakuro
message: "Yes, there was. There was a person that I had to meet, no matter what."

name: Zakuro
message: "And I remembered the legend."

name: Yuki
message: "What legend?"

name: Zakuro
message: "About the place where the girl of the sky meets the girl who is the world itself."

name: Zakuro
message: "That legend has become a sort of spell on this town."

name: Yuki
message: "A spell?"

name: Zakuro
message: "It's famous. So you really didn't know. It's a spell that lets you find the person you love."

name: Yuki
message: "I see. So then, the last person you wanted to meet was the person you loved."

name: Zakuro
message: "Yes."

name: Yuki
message: "Then you shouldn't be trying to use a charm or anything. If you asked us, we could've helped you find him."

name: Zakuro
message: "No, there's no need to search."

name: Yuki
message: "Huh? What do you mean, no need?"

name: Zakuro
message: "You see, the spell worked properly."

name: Yuki
message: "It worked? You mean you managed to find the person you were looking for?"

name: Zakuro
message: "Yes, thanks to you."

name: Yuki
message: "B-But isn't that a bit strange? You say you found the person you loved, but you spent the entire time at my place, right? What exactly were you doing?"

name: Zakuro
message: "Because the person I wanted to meet was Yuki-sama. It was you."

name: Yuki
message: "Me?"

name: Zakuro
message: "Yes."

name: Yuki
message: "Um, let me get this straight. Uh, first off, we're both girls, and besides, you didn't even know me until after you dropped the doll on my head."

name: Zakuro
message: "That's how it seems to you, but that's just because you're so forgetful."

name: Zakuro
message: "If my being a girl is a problem, you should say that to the other two, not just me."

name: Yuki
message: "By the other two, you mean..."

name: Zakuro
message: "Kagami-san and Tsukasa-san."

name: Yuki
message: "Um, they were probably just playing along, you know?"

name: Zakuro
message: "Maybe so. They probably were."

name: Zakuro
message: "But when someone falls in love with another person, that's just how it is. Or do you think falling in love is something intentional?"

name: Yuki
message: "Uh, no... That's not it. How can I put it?"

name: Zakuro
message: "I had a lot of fun the last few days."

name: Zakuro
message: "At first I thought that I only wanted to be with you because I like you, but as the days went by, I realized that I like Kagami-san and Tsukasa-san too."

name: Zakuro
message: "I loved being together with you all."

name: Yuki
message: "Takashima-san..."

name: Zakuro
message: "Competing with Kagami-san for you, playing with Tsukasa-san..."

name: Zakuro
message: "So thank you for everything."
The legend that tells of the meeting between the world girl and the sky girl.
The legend that led to some kind of spell for the two girls.
The sky we were going to search for was in Takashima-san's hand from the moment we started to search.
That was the answer.

name: Zakuro
message: "And... I'm sorry."

name: Yuki
message: "You're sorry?"

name: Zakuro
message: "Maybe I shouldn't say this."

name: Yuki
message: "What's wrong, Takashima-san?"

name: Zakuro
message: "Tomorrow, when we go stargazing, let's start right after midnight."

name: Yuki
message: "Right after midnight? You mean right when the date changes to the 20th?"

name: Zakuro
message: "Yes."

name: Yuki
message: "Why? If we want to find the sky, it would be easier to do at an earlier time. And besides, we can't really be in the building that late."

name: Zakuro
message: "There's no problem. We can get into the building."

name: Yuki
message: "Huh?"

name: Zakuro
message: "If the world girl desires it, nothing in this world is impossible."

name: Yuki
message: "Uh, excuse me. Takashima-san, by that..."

name: Zakuro
message: "I understand. It sounds like I'm saying something strange to you right now. You probably think I'm some 'mysterious girl,' or maybe that I'm some 'crazy weirdo'..."

name: Yuki
message: "Ah, no, that's not..."

name: Zakuro
message: "It's fine. I understand now. I understand everything."

name: Zakuro
message: "At first, I suffered for my own sin."

name: Yuki
message: "Your own sin?"

name: Zakuro
message: "Yes. I thought that I had to atone for the crime I'd committed."

name: Yuki
message: "Some kind of redemption?"

name: Zakuro
message: "But I understand after walking through this world together with you."

name: Zakuro
message: "The redemption that I seek will only end up making you suffer."

name: Zakuro
message: "If I don't do it, then you'll be happy. You can live the rest of your life like this, in this Wonderful Everyday. But..."

name: Zakuro
message: "My redemption would take that all away."

name: Yuki
message: "U-Uh, I don't really get it."

name: Zakuro
message: "Tomorrow, before the 19th ends, please come to Building C."

name: Yuki
message: "Um, then, Kagami and Tsukasa..."

name: Zakuro
message: "If you wish, I think you'll be able to bring them along. But if you don't wish to, you won't be able to."

name: Yuki
message: "What does that..."

name: Zakuro
message: "You'll understand tomorrow."

name: Zakuro
message: "Depending on how you approach the day we call tomorrow, the shape of the world could change greatly."

name: Yuki
message: "The day we call tomorrow?"

name: Zakuro
message: "Yes, just like this shop..."

name: Yuki
message: "This shop is..."
Then it hit me. The other customers were gone. Even the bartender was nowhere to be seen.
The only two people here were Takashima-san and me.

name: Yuki
message: "Th-This is..."

name: Zakuro
message: "Most likely, it's because you wish for a long journey."

name: Yuki
message: "A long journey?"

name: Zakuro
message: "Yes. So, tomorrow, on the rooftop..."

name: Yuki
message: "Wait, Takashima-san!"
